title: Pokemon Detail Validation, Derived Types, Units, and Inclusive Presentation · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript Pokedex interaction contains one plausible readline, token, type, validation, URL, fetch, promise, cache, abort, persistence, security, test, package, or release defect; find the first runtime or compile-time boundary failure before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in validated inclusive Pokemon detail card and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Validate numeric ID or constrained canonical name and retain both requested input and resolved resource identity.
Validate ID, name, types, abilities, stats, height, weight, sprites or references, bounds, and member shapes from unknown.
Map transport fields into a readonly discriminated view that separates available, empty, and unsupported information.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 6787 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate Pokemon detail JSON, derived view types, units, ordered sections, and inclusive output; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use budget must remain enforced; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate numeric ID or constrained canonical name and retain both requested input and resolved resource identity.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-ts-detail-accessibility-tests-1lqad1" that uses case 3901 to validate Pokemon detail JSON, derived view types, units, ordered sections, and inclusive output.
